archetype: Seed founder with no marketing hire
trigger: Founder-led team; sporadic LinkedIn; little or no SEO base; wants visible presence without overbuilding
rationale: Matches the public entry retainer for seed-stage B2B SaaS founders who need founder presence plus a light SEO base before a larger content system. Start with the pilot, then Foundation is the cleanest next step.

archetype: Very early founder still sharpening ICP and message
trigger: Pre-revenue or low traction; unclear positioning; asks for strategy before volume
rationale: The need is upstream clarity, not monthly content volume. A scoped diagnostic is safer than forcing a retainer too early; Foundation can follow once the message is clearer.

archetype: Series A team needing organic pipeline
trigger: PMF signals; active blog or resource center; founder willing to participate; lead pressure from content
add_on_fit: Second founder LinkedIn ghostwriting; Content audit
rationale: This is the clearest Engine fit: founder brand plus company SEO working together to generate pipeline, with room to expand once attribution and distribution become priorities.

archetype: GTM-led company that wants demos from content
trigger: Webinars, product launches, case studies, and pipeline language are already visible
rationale: The buyer already thinks in GTM terms, so Engine is the strongest commercial bridge from content to pipeline. Full Stack becomes relevant when they need tighter measurement and broader distribution.

archetype: Technical product needing clearer market-facing execution
trigger: Complex product narrative; frequent feature rollouts; lean team; content exists but lacks translation
add_on_fit: Positioning + messaging audit; SEO technical audit
rationale: These companies usually need stronger translation from product depth to buyer-facing content. Engine fits the recurring execution need, while Full Stack is the upgrade if technical SEO and attribution also matter.

archetype: Technical company ready for authority assets and measurement
trigger: Active resource center; subject-matter depth; board or revenue accountability; CRM or GA4 access
rationale: This segment can support the heavier research, technical SEO, and attribution layers inside Full Stack. Engine is the fallback when the team is not yet ready to operationalize the full measurement stack.

archetype: Series B team that must prove content ROI
trigger: Leadership asks how content drives demos and pipeline; multiple stakeholders; existing content motion
add_on_fit: GEO; pSEO setup; Second founder LinkedIn ghostwriting
rationale: Full Stack is the best fit when content has to be defended as a growth lever, not a line item. The added attribution and distribution layers matter more here than simple content volume.

archetype: Company with large integration, use-case, or comparison surface
trigger: Many product pages or templates to expand; strong SEO opportunity beyond editorial content
rationale: The retainer should be anchored in Full Stack if they also need the core content machine; pSEO is the natural add-on. If they only want the build, route it through a scoped project first.

archetype: Founder already active but company content is weak
trigger: Founder has a point of view and some traction; brand page and blog are inconsistent or underpowered
add_on_fit: Content audit; Second founder LinkedIn ghostwriting
rationale: Foundation would underbuild the opportunity because the founder signal already exists. Engine is the better fit when the gap is turning isolated thought leadership into a company-level content system.

archetype: Agency that needs white-label execution capacity
trigger: Overflow delivery; multi-client load; asks for backend content, SEO, social, or reporting support
rationale: This is not a clean fit for the public SaaS tiers. The right commercial route is a secondary custom capacity track or a scoped white-label arrangement built around delivery reliability.

archetype: Founder-led service firm with active marketing but lean team
trigger: Visible LinkedIn and blog motion; wants consistency and organic demand but does not need paid media
rationale: This is an adjacent ICP, not the homepage core, so Foundation is safer unless the team already behaves like a small content machine. Engine becomes more credible when content is clearly tied to pipeline goals.

archetype: Established service firm with strong content motion
trigger: Case studies, webinars, active brand presence, and pressure to turn content into pipeline
rationale: When the need looks like recurring authority plus demand capture, Engine can work even outside SaaS. Confidence stays below core SaaS because the public positioning is still B2B SaaS-first.

archetype: Lean marketing team with strong content need
trigger: Resource center, webinars, subject-matter depth, and founder or expert voice available
rationale: EdTech can fit the Engine model when the motion is content-heavy and expert authority matters. It is adjacent, so the package hypothesis should still be validated in the pilot.

archetype: Brand whose real need is content and social execution, not paid ads
trigger: Active organic and social motion; clear need for execution support; no request for performance marketing or video
rationale: This sits outside the cleanest public package narrative. A scoped project or capacity-style scope is safer than bending the SaaS tier structure around a lower-priority segment.
